market-development.title: Market Development
market-development.prompt: Existing product × new market. Take the same offering to a new geography, segment, or use occasion. Risk: medium — the product is proven, the customer is not.
examples: Geographic expansion to a new country
Same product targeted at a new B2B segment
Existing service reframed for a new use occasion

diversification.title: Diversification
diversification.prompt: New product × new market. Both axes move. Risk: highest — usually only viable if the core is mature and there is real adjacency, or if both customer and offer share at least one substrate.
examples: Amazon launching AWS (new product + new buyer)

market-penetration.title: Market Penetration
market-penetration.prompt: Existing product × existing market. Sell more to customers you already serve. Risk: lowest. Levers: usage, repeat purchase, share-of-wallet, win-back, pricing optimisation.
examples: Loyalty program to lift repeat purchase

product-development.title: Product Development
product-development.prompt: New product × existing market. Build new offerings for customers you already understand. Risk: medium — the buyer relationship is proven, the offer is not.
examples: New product line sold through current channels
Service add-on for current customer base

growth-rationale.title: Growth rationale
growth-rationale.prompt: For the chosen quadrant, name (a) what stays the same, (b) what must change in the BMC, (c) the riskiest assumption per candidate, and (d) the capacity to actually do this in parallel with everything else.
examples: Keep current production base; change channels to D2C; risky if D2C unit economics fail at scale
Reuse customer-success motion; add new product line; risky if churn shifts upward
